Double Major – Minor

Click for Double Major Program Infographic.

Double Major Program

The aim of the Double Major Program is to enable students who want to study in a second major to obtain a second bachelor’s degree while continuing their major undergraduate program with outstanding success.

Application and Admission Conditions

1.

The criteria that students who want to apply to Double Major Programs are expected;

The Major Program must be at the beginning of the third semester at the earliest and at the beginning of the fifth semester at the latest

To have a minimum GPA of at least 2.72 out of 4.00

To be in the 20% in terms of success

– ÖSYM students who are not in the 20% are expected to have a Central Placement Score (ÖSYM), which is not less than the base score of the program in which the Double Major Program will be applied.

Having succeeded in all the courses taken in the Major Program

– A student who has received an FF grade in XXX, then re-takes this course and succeeded with a CC grade can apply. The important thing is the final grade of the courses.

2.

A student who wants to do a double major in a program that requires a success ranking (Law, Engineering, Architecture, etc.); The relevant program must meet the Placement Exam(YKS) success ranking requirement determined by the Council of Higher Education for the year in which the student is registered.

Students who come from abroad through external transfer or are accepted and registered from abroad cannot do a double major in programs that require success ranking (Law, Engineering, Architecture and similar).

3.

Students who study in the Theater Department and want to apply for a Double Major in another department are expected to meet the base score of our University in the score type of the relevant department and the success ranking requirement determined by the Council of Higher Education.

4.

Students who meet the criteria mentioned above can make their Double Major Program applications online through MyKhas Portal.

5.

Applications are evaluated by the Dean’s Office of the relevant Faculty.

6.

Admission procedures for Double Major Programs are submitted to the Student Affairs Directorate with the decision of the Faculty Administrative Board regarding the Department’s recommendation of the Double Major Program applied for, and registration is done.

7.

The scholarships of the students who attend a Double Major Program shall proceed for five years, ten semesters without interruption as of the semester they started the double major program.

8.

It is not possible to enroll in more than one double major program at the same time. However, it is possible to enroll in a double major program and a minor program at the same time.

Click for Minor Program Infographic.

Minor Program

The aim of the Minor Program is to ensure that students complete a limited number of courses on a specific subject and qualify for the Minor Certificate.

Application and Admission Conditions

1.

The criteria that students who want to apply to Minor Programs are expected;

The Major Program must be completed at the beginning of the third semester at the earliest and at the beginning of the sixth semester at the latest

To have a minimum GPA of at least 2.50 out of 4.00

Having succeeded in all the courses taken in the Major Program

– A student who has received an FF grade in XXX, then re-takes this course and succeeded with a CC grade can apply. The important thing is the final grade of the courses.

2.

Students who meet the criteria mentioned above can make their Minor Program applications online through MyKhas Portal.

3.

Applications are evaluated by the Dean’s Office of the relevant Faculty.

4.

Admission procedures for Minor Programs are submitted to the Student Affairs Directorate with the decision of the Faculty Administrative Board regarding the Department’s recommendation of the Minor Program applied for, and registration is done.

5.

It is not possible to enroll in more than one double major program at the same time. However, it is possible to enroll in a double major program and a minor program at the same time. A student whose registration has been canceled from the minor program cannot re-register for the same minor program.

6.

Students currently enrolled in a minor program can apply to another minor program, if they provided to meet the application conditions.
